executive_summary:
Q1 2026 was a quarter of meaningful platform acceleration paired with a visible trust and usability tax. On the product/engineering side, ElizaOS made clear progress toward an “agent internet”: a public agent ecosystem roadmap (discovery, stable URLs, forking, knowledge-sharing), major runtime improvements (MultiStep performance refactor, parallel action execution, multi-user session management, streaming, prompt batching), and a step-change in tool interoperability via dynamic MCP actions. March’s launch of identity + payment primitives (AgentID/SAID, x402, multi-chain USDC rails) moved ElizaOS from “agents that can act” toward “agents that can transact safely,” unlocking credible agent-commerce demos.

In parallel, the quarter was dominated socially by the token migration and operationally by onboarding/billing friction in ElizaCloud—both of which materially influenced sentiment and adoption. Migration support delays, eligibility edge cases, zero-balance reports, and scam pressure created a trust deficit that persisted after the deadline. Meanwhile, continued reports of high skill invocation failure rates and release/branch confusion undermined developer confidence precisely as the roadmap expanded. The net: the product surface area is growing quickly, but leadership attention must shift from adding capabilities to making the stack reliable, compatible, and frictionless end-to-end (developer → deploy → transact).

accomplishments: Established a concrete roadmap for public agent discovery, standardized URLs, forking, and cross-agent knowledge sharing (issues #6302–#6305).
Increased registry momentum (wallet + swarm capability expansion), signaling ecosystem pull and partner interest.
key_achievements.impact: Set the direction for agents as the primary product interface (“front door”), enabling discoverability and reuse as the growth flywheel.

key_achievements.theme: Runtime Performance, Concurrency, and Cost Efficiency
accomplishments: Refactored the default message service provider handling for MultiStep actions, improving complex task throughput (PR #6263).
Shipped Parallel Action Execution, multi-user Session Manager, and streaming responses to reduce latency and improve responsiveness under concurrency.
Implemented prompt batching to improve cloud economics (lower cost per task) while maintaining quality.
key_achievements.impact: Advanced ElizaOS from single-threaded bot behavior toward production-grade, multi-user agent runtime—critical for Cloud viability and flagship demos.

accomplishments: Merged unified hooks with multi-transport support (HTTP/SSE/WebSocket), reducing integration fragmentation across clients.
Fixed CLI/create flows and corrected install guidance to @elizaos/cli, restoring first-time project generation.
Improved social integration security via Twitter Broker Authentication.
Expanded documentation coverage, improving self-serve builder success and reducing support burden.
key_achievements.impact: Reduced the activation energy for builders and integrators; created a more coherent “golden path” foundation (though still incomplete).

key_achievements.theme: Tooling Architecture Leap: MCP Dynamic Actions
accomplishments: Merged the v1.8.0 dynamic MCP tool actions update (>4k LOC), enabling more flexible tool invocation patterns and future interoperability.
key_achievements.impact: Raised the ceiling for composable tools/skills and external ecosystem integration; positioned ElizaOS to be a “tool router” for agent systems.

accomplishments: Introduced AgentID and integrated SAID for verifiable, on-chain agent identity and signatures (agent “passports”).
Implemented x402 payments, marketplace hooks (MAXIA), and multi-chain USDC rails (Coin Railz) with payment safety checks and gas automation.
key_achievements.impact: Unlocked credible end-to-end commerce demos and the next platform narrative—provided safety/compatibility standards are enforced.

persistent_challenges.issue: Trust and sentiment damage from token migration (support delays, edge cases, zero-balance reports, scams)
persistent_challenges.root_cause: Operational readiness gap (support capacity, wallet edge-case handling, eligibility/detection reliability) combined with hard deadlines and insufficient anti-scam/expectations management.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Run a structured trust recovery program: publish a post-mortem, define remediation paths (including clear criteria for any manual intervention), ship wallet/eligibility diagnostics, and maintain an always-on anti-scam comms cadence.

persistent_challenges.issue: Reliability gaps in skills/tool invocation and concerns about malicious skills
persistent_challenges.root_cause: Weak activation discipline and guardrails (defaults allow fragile configurations), limited automated testing/harness coverage, and insufficient sandboxing/review for third-party skills.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Establish a reliability baseline program: deterministic activation rules, test harnesses per skill category, sandboxing, signed manifests, and automated security review for registry submissions.

persistent_challenges.issue: Cloud onboarding, billing friction, and pricing opacity blocking adoption
persistent_challenges.root_cause: End-to-end customer journey not owned as a product: fragmented UX, payment/VPN friction, and unclear pricing/credits mechanics.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Create a Cloud Growth Reliability squad with weekly KPIs (activation, conversion, support tickets). Ship pricing transparency, fix known onboarding blockers, and standardize “developer → deploy” guided flows.

persistent_challenges.issue: Compatibility, release-train confusion, and plugin breakage perception (v2 alpha vs 1.x, branches, migration path)
persistent_challenges.root_cause: No explicit compatibility contract or stable release trains; insufficient tooling and messaging for upgrades; ecosystem moving faster than version governance.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Publish a compatibility contract (API stability promises, deprecation windows), provide migration tooling, and run a predictable release train with “known-good” stacks.

persistent_challenges.issue: Narrative fragmentation and prioritization ambiguity (Babylon/agent-social vs infra-first, Jeju, marketplace, flagship agents)
persistent_challenges.root_cause: Multiple top-level bets competing for resources without a single primary success metric and milestone cadence.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Pick one primary bet for the next 6–8 weeks (with weekly milestones), keep other tracks in “maintenance mode,” and align comms so the market sees one coherent story.

persistent_challenges.issue: Database refactor risk during ecosystem acceleration
persistent_challenges.root_cause: Core architectural debt colliding with rapid feature shipping; risk of destabilizing production during high change velocity.
persistent_challenges.recommendation: Timebox the refactor with a hard “stabilize first” gate, invest in migration tooling + performance benchmarks, and ship behind feature flags with rollback plans.

improved.progress: Fixes landed in February restoring project generation and correcting installation guidance to @elizaos/cli, removing a high-friction foot-gun.
MultiStep performance refactor (Jan) followed by parallel action execution, multi-user sessions, streaming, and prompt batching (Mar) materially improved latency/cost and scalability.
Unified hooks with HTTP/SSE/WebSocket reduced fragmentation and simplified integrations, enabling more consistent client implementations.
AgentID/SAID identity and x402 + multi-chain USDC rails shipped in March, enabling verifiable identity and safer payments primitives.

stagnant.blocker: Non-technical remediation and communications did not keep pace with user stressors (deadline pressure, support delays, scam pressure, zero-balance confusion). Trust requires process, not just code.

stagnant.issue: ElizaCloud onboarding/payment friction and pricing transparency
stagnant.blocker: No single accountable funnel owner and insufficient end-to-end instrumentation; fixes remain reactive rather than KPI-driven.

stagnant.issue: Skill invocation reliability and security posture
stagnant.blocker: Missing standardized activation discipline, automated test harnesses, and strong sandbox/review pipelines for skills—leading to recurring failure and malicious-skill concerns.
Lack of a published compatibility contract and predictable release train; ecosystem perceives breakage as a platform property rather than a transitional state.

strategic_recommendations.area: Reliability + Compatibility as a first-class product (Core + Skills + Plugins)
strategic_recommendations.rationale: The quarter’s biggest drag was not missing features—it was inconsistent execution: skill failures, upgrade confusion, and breakage perception. Commerce and public agents only compound if developers can trust the stack.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Known-good stack published; plugin/skill compatibility matrix; skill success rate ≥ 95% on a standard benchmark suite; predictable release train with documented deprecations and migration tooling.

strategic_recommendations.area: Cloud onboarding & billing transparency (own the funnel)
strategic_recommendations.rationale: Cloud is currently a trust bottleneck and adoption limiter; it also underpins token utility narratives. Fixing onboarding and pricing clarity converts interest into sustained usage.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Activation rate +30% QoQ; onboarding support tickets -50%; pricing page and in-product cost estimates aligned; time-to-first-successful-deploy < 15 minutes for the golden path.

strategic_recommendations.area: Token migration trust recovery + always-on anti-scam operations
strategic_recommendations.rationale: Migration created lasting reputational damage. Without an explicit recovery plan, every new product launch inherits distrust and support load.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Published migration post-mortem + remediation policy; measurable reduction in migration-related tickets/scam reports; weekly comms cadence; audited eligibility/wallet diagnostics available to users.

strategic_recommendations.area: Public Agent Discovery MVP (narrow scope, ship fast)
strategic_recommendations.rationale: Public agents are the distribution flywheel, but must be tangible: search, stable URLs, and a minimal fork/clone flow. Overbuilding governance/monetization will delay the front door.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Searchable agent directory + stable agent URLs; one-click fork/clone into a runnable template; basic provenance (signed publisher identity) and reporting tools.

strategic_recommendations.rationale: With payments shipped, failures become financial. Safety primitives turn commerce from demos into platform trust.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Default allowlists/blocklists; transaction simulation + policy checks; audit trails; optional escrow; clear user confirmations and rollback/compensation policies where applicable.

strategic_recommendations.area: One flagship end-to-end demo: discover → hire → deliver → attest → pay
strategic_recommendations.rationale: ElizaOS needs a single narrative anchor that exercises identity, discovery, runtime, and payments in one coherent story—creating a reference implementation for docs and partners.
strategic_recommendations.success_criteria: Public demo + reproducible repo; documented APIs; measurable success rate; uses AgentID/SAID and x402 rails; becomes the default tutorial path.

current_gaps: “Execution Excellence” is interpreted as runtime performance, but not consistently extended to migration ops, onboarding, billing clarity, and support readiness.
No explicit emphasis on compatibility/upgrade clarity despite rapid ecosystem change and plugin breakage perception.
Commerce primitives shipped without an equally prominent safety-by-default doctrine.
suggested_additions: Treat onboarding, billing, and token operations as production-critical reliability surfaces (not community/support side quests).
Add a formal “Compatibility & Upgrade Clarity” clause: stable release trains, plugin contracts, and migration tooling.
Elevate “Safety by Default for Economic Actions” as a core requirement (guardrails, auditability, and user protection).
Clarify stack relationship: Framework is open base; Cloud is default deploy path with transparent pricing; flagship apps are proofs—not competing priorities.
north_star_evolution.proposed_revision: North Star (revised): Build the most reliable, composable agent platform—where developers can go from code to deploy to commerce with minimal friction. Execution Excellence includes: (1) frictionless onboarding and transparent operations (Cloud, billing, token/wallet flows, documentation), (2) compatibility and upgrade clarity (stable release trains, plugin/skill contracts, migration tooling), and (3) safety by default for economic actions (identity, payments, guardrails, audit trails). Public agents are the front door; Cloud is the default runway; flagship agents prove the stack end-to-end.

metrics_to_track.metric: Skill/tool invocation success rate (P50/P95) on a standardized benchmark suite
metrics_to_track.why: Direct measure of real agent reliability; addresses the reported high failure rates and unlocks ecosystem trust.
metrics_to_track.target: ≥ 95% overall success rate; P95 latency within defined budgets per skill class by end of Q2 2026

metrics_to_track.why: Captures whether DX + Cloud onboarding are actually frictionless.
metrics_to_track.target: < 15 minutes median; < 30 minutes P90 by end of Q2 2026

metrics_to_track.metric: Cloud funnel conversion (sign-up → deploy → retained week-4) with ticket rate per 100 users
metrics_to_track.why: Quantifies onboarding/payment friction and support load; forces ownership of the end-to-end journey.
metrics_to_track.target: Week-4 retention +20% QoQ; onboarding/billing tickets -50% by end of Q2 2026

metrics_to_track.metric: Release train health: % of plugins passing compatibility tests on release day
metrics_to_track.why: Turns upgrade clarity into an enforceable contract; reduces breakage perception.
metrics_to_track.target: ≥ 90% of top plugins (by installs) green on release day; published deprecation windows for all breaking changes

metrics_to_track.metric: Public agent discovery adoption: searchable listings, forks, and successful runs from a fork
metrics_to_track.why: Validates the “agents as front door” flywheel with measurable engagement.
metrics_to_track.target: Discovery MVP shipped; ≥ 1,000 monthly searches; ≥ 200 forks/month; ≥ 60% of forks achieve a successful run within 1 hour by end of Q2 2026

metrics_to_track.metric: Commerce safety: failed/blocked transactions rate + incident count (scams, mispays) + mean time to resolution
metrics_to_track.why: Ensures economic actions are safe-by-default as payments mature from demo to production.
metrics_to_track.target: < 0.5% preventable payment incidents; MTTR < 48 hours; audit trails available for 100% of commerce flows

metrics_to_track.metric: Trust recovery: migration-related ticket volume and sentiment index (community channels) over time
metrics_to_track.why: Migration aftermath is reputational debt; tracking ensures the recovery plan is working.
metrics_to_track.target: Migration-related tickets -70% vs Q1 baseline; sustained positive/neutral sentiment majority by end of Q2 2026
